title: Actinocephalus callophyllus
taxonomy: Actinocephalus callophyllus is a species within the family Eriocaulaceae. This family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as pipeworts. They are native to tropical and subtropical regions of South America. The classification of Actinocephalus callophyllus is further categorized as part of the genus Actinocephalus, which is a monotypic genus. The Eriocaulaceae family is also part of the order Poales, which includes various families of grasses and other monocots.

intro: Actinocephalus callophyllus is a species of flowering plant in the family Eriocaulaceae, commonly known as a type of fimbriate sedge. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, offering potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in the management of various health conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress-related diseases. The bioactive constituents of Actinocephalus callophyllus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Actinocephalus callophyllus is commonly prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it is also used in the form of essential oil and dried leaves for topical applications.
